data.title: Sun Life Financial Agrees to Buy Bell Partners for $350 Million
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Sun Life Financial agreed to acquire Bell Partners for $350 million.

The financial services company said at least 75% of the purchase price will be payable in stock. It said Bell Partners would become the company's U.S. multifamily operating platform, adding that the acquisition is expected to be accretive to underlying earnings per share in 2026.

Bell Partners will retain its current leadership and property-level branding, Sun Life said. It will operate under BGO, Sun Life's global real estate investment management adviser and real estate services provider.

The Bell Partners deal is expected to close in the second half of the year.

Sun Life also said Monday that it had closed the acquisition of the remaining equity interests in both BGO and Crescent Capital Group, a global alternative credit investment manager.

Sun Life paid $1.59 billion Canadian dollars (about $1.16 billion) for the remaining 44% stake in BGO, as well as C$829 million for the remaining 49% stake in Crescent. The payments were funded through debt issuances in 2025, the company said.

The company expects to report a C$236 million charge and a C$85 million reduction in equity in its first quarter results related to the transactions.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Corn barge basis steady-firm on solid export demand
data.description:
Spot basis bids for corn and soybeans shipped by barge to U.S. Gulf Coast terminals were mostly steady to firm on Monday, underpinned by moderate exporter demand and slow grain sales by farmers, traders said.

  • Corn sales to river elevators have slowed with a recent drop in prices, although demand from exporters remained brisk. Chicago Board of Trade corn futures fell to a 1-1/2 week low on Monday while soybeans firmed, ahead of a U.S. Department of Agriculture plantings report on Tuesday.

  • The USDA reported 145,000 tons in new corn export sales to undisclosed buyers on Monday.

  • The USDA said 1,789,524 metric tons of U.S. corn were inspected for export in the week ended March 26, above trade estimates for up to 1,650,000 tons.

  • Soybean inspections totaled 586,427 tons, below trade estimates for at least 600,000 tons, USDA data showed.

  • U.S. soybean export demand was seasonally slow as Brazilian supplies are available at lower prices. Traders are looking ahead to a mid-May U.S.-China summit that could spark Chinese government purchases of U.S. crops.

  • Brazilian farmers had harvested 75% of their 2025/26 soybean crop as of last Thursday, agribusiness consultancy AgRural said on Monday.

  • CIF Gulf corn barges loaded in March were bid a penny higher at 88 cents over CBOT May (CK26) futures. April barge bids were up 3 cents at 88 cents over futures.

  • FOB corn offers for vessels loaded in April were unchanged around 96 cents over futures.

  • CIF Gulf soybean barges loaded in March were bid 1 cent higher at 72 cents over CBOT May (SK26) soybean futures. April barges were bid steady at 74 cents over futures.

  • FOB export premiums for April soybean vessels were unchanged at around 92 cents over futures.

data.title: Brazil's Natura announces stake purchase by Advent as founders exit board
data.description:
Brazilian cosmetics maker Natura said on Monday that U.S. private equity firm Advent has signed a binding agreement to buy a stake in the company, while also announcing a board reshuffle that includes the departure of its founders.

  • Advent International will buy between 8% and 10% of Natura for an average price target of 9.75 reais ($1.86) per share, Natura said in a securities filing.

  • The deal will be carried out in the secondary market over a six-month period.

  • Advent will be entitled to appoint two members to Natura's board of directors.

BOARD SHAKE-UP

  • Natura also announced changes to its board of directors, with founders Luiz Seabra, Guilherme Leal and Pedro Passos stepping down.

  • Chairman and former Chief Executive Fabio Barbosa will also leave his seat.

  • Natura has proposed to shareholders that the four form a new advisory board, with no decision-making powers.

  • Under the proposal, Alessandro Carlucci would become chairman.

($1 = 5.2520 reais)

data.title: Haymaker Acquisition 2025 10-K: Net Loss of $(1.47)M, Trust Interest Income $10.37M
data.description:
Haymaker Acquisition reported a net loss of $1.47 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, compared with net income of $11.32 million in 2024; the 2025 result reflects interest income largely offset by fair‑value adjustments to PIPE liabilities and general and administrative expenses. Interest earned on investments held in the company’s Trust Account was $10.37 million in 2025 (and $12.26 million in 2024), which the filing notes as non‑operating income available to pay taxes. The company is pursuing a multi‑step business combination with Suncrete and has secured incremental PIPE commitments and governance arrangements to support closing and post‑close operations.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income (Loss), year ended Dec 31, 2025: $(1.47) million (net loss; interest income offset by PIPE liability fair value adjustments and G&A).
  • Net Income, year ended Dec 31, 2024: $11.32 million (primarily interest earned on Trust Account).
  • Interest earned on Trust Account investments: $10.37 million (2025) and $12.26 million (2024); characterized as non‑operating income available to pay taxes.

Business Highlights

  • Business combination strategy: Pursuing a Suncrete business combination involving multi‑step domestication and mergers to transition to an operating public company (PubCo) and the target’s operational structure.
  • Capital deployment and PIPE support: Secured incremental PIPE commitments totaling $167.1 million and executed Non‑Redemption Agreements intended to preserve cash available for post‑close operations.
  • Operational readiness and use of proceeds: Preparing to use Trust Account proceeds together with PIPE funds to finance target operations, due diligence, integration and growth initiatives after closing.
  • Management and governance changes: Filed board and executive updates (including removal of the president) and established sponsor agreements to support consummation of the transaction and governance of the combined entity.

Original SEC Filing: Haymaker Acquisition Corp. 4 [ HYAC ] - 10-K - Mar. 30, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Aspire Biopharma 10-K: Revenue $6.2K, Net Loss $24.48M
data.description:
Aspire Biopharma reported minimal net revenue of $0.006202 million (approximately $6,202) for the period, with no per‑share net loss disclosed in Part 2 Item 7/Item 8. The company recorded a net loss of $24.480848 million driven by operating losses and significant interest and fair value charges. Gross margin was negative and operations generated a loss from continuing operations of $19.351291 million.

Financial Highlights

  • Net revenue: $0.006202 million (approximately $6,202); company commenced product sales in 2025.
  • Gross margin: $(0.116) thousand (negative gross margin due to cost of revenue exceeding revenue).
  • Loss from operations: $(19.351291) million.
  • Net loss: $(24.480848) million, which includes significant interest expense and fair value changes.
  • Net loss per share: Not reported as a per‑share figure in Part 2 Item 7/Item 8; only total net loss disclosed.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue initiation and channels: Began product sales in Q3 2025 with initial commercial launch into a nutraceutical channel and direct‑to‑consumer activity.
  • Go‑to‑market and brand activity: Launched the Buzz Bomb caffeine nutraceutical, pursued direct‑to‑consumer sales, and exhibited at major fitness conventions to build brand awareness.
  • Manufacturing commitments: Secured 2 million‑unit manufacturing commitment for Buzz Bomb for 2026 and engaged CDMOs (Glatt, Microsize) for cGMP batch production and stability testing.
  • R&D and clinical progress: Reported positive July 2025 PK/PD results for a high‑dose sublingual aspirin program and received FDA pre‑IND feedback supporting a planned ~32‑subject follow‑on trial.
  • Pipeline expansion: Advanced formulation efforts and stability testing across additional nutraceutical and supplement formulations (melatonin, vitamins, erectile dysfunction and others).

Original SEC Filing: Aspire Biopharma Holdings, Inc. [ ASBP ] - 10-K - Mar. 30, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Applied Energetics 2025 10-K: Revenue $0.46M, Net loss $14.87M
data.description:
Applied Energetics reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $0.462 million alongside a net loss of $14.873 million, driven by the suspension of two major government contracts and increased operating expenses. Revenue declined sharply year-over-year while losses widened as the company ramped internal R&D and expanded operational capacity. The results reflect a transition from contract-driven revenue to internally funded development and demonstration activities.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0.462 million (reported as $461,727), down 81% year-over-year due to suspension of two customer contracts.
  • Gross Profit: $0.249 million (as implied in the filing).
  • Net Income: Net loss of $(14.873) million (reported as $(14,872,730)), a 62% increase in net loss year-over-year driven by higher G&A, S&M, R&D and lower revenue.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: The company shifted from contract revenue to internally funded R&D and demonstrations after DoD funding for key contracts was suspended, causing the steep revenue decline in 2025.
  • Operational Scale-up: Applied Energetics opened a Battle Lab, expanded leased laboratory and manufacturing space, and installed USP testing equipment to support field demonstrations.
  • Product & Testing Milestones: Integrated USP technology onto the Kord FIREFLY platform and completed multiple field tests, including a demonstration that disabled a drone sensor.
  • R&D Momentum: Internal research and commercialization efforts ramped up, with increased staffing, expanded testing activities, and partnerships with universities to advance technology development.
  • Channel Shift: Business focus moved toward demonstrations and commercialization activities following the loss of Department of Defense funding for previously active contracts.

Original SEC Filing: APPLIED ENERGETICS, INC. [ AERG ] - 10-K - Mar. 30, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Fed's Williams energy price view driven by market pricing
data.description:
By Michael S. Derby

Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said Monday his view that energy prices will retreat later this year is driven by the financial market’s outlook.

Williams told reporters after a speech in Staten Island, New York, that his outlook is “kind of consistent with where markets see oil prices, but there's obviously, you know, other scenarios.” He added: “We’ll have to see over…coming weeks” how this plays out.

While this happens, he said, "I just have to watch the data and try to get better understanding” of what the surge in energy prices will do to the economy.

data.title: BullFrog AI Enters One-Year Feasibility Pact With Global Pharma to Advance MDD Targets
data.description:
BullFrog AI entered into a Feasibility Agreement with an undisclosed global pharmaceutical company to apply its bfLEAP platform to identify and prioritize drug targets for major depressive disorder. The one-year engagement includes milestone payments tied to deliverables such as prioritized targets, causal gene networks, and target dossiers. The client may obtain an exclusive right to use a selected final target for three years and can terminate without cause on 30 days’ notice; standard breach remedies apply.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Feasibility Agreement for AI-driven drug target discovery (MDD)
  • Counterparty: Undisclosed Global Pharmaceutical Company
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 27 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: 1 year
  • Reason: Apply bfLEAP to identify MDD drug targets

Original SEC Filing: BullFrog AI Holdings, Inc. [ BFRG ] - 8-K - Mar. 30, 2026

data.title: Advanced Flower Capital Lifts Revolver to $106 Million, Including Temporary $26 Million Increase
data.description:
Advanced Flower Capital entered into Amendment Number Eight to its Loan and Security Agreement to expand its revolving credit capacity. The amendment raises aggregate revolver commitments by $56 million to $106 million, comprising a $30 million permanent increase and a $26 million temporary increase effective Mar 27–Apr 10, 2026. After the temporary period, total commitments will reduce to $80 million. The company expects the added capacity to enhance liquidity for near-term deployment.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Amendment to revolving credit facility under Loan and Security Agreement
  • Counterparty: Lenders and Administrative Agent
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 27 2026 / same
  • Reason: Increase liquidity to support loan originations and portfolio growth

Original SEC Filing: Advanced Flower Capital Inc. [ AFCG ] - 8-K - Mar. 30, 2026

data.title: Northern Star's New Bull Argues Case for Asset Sales — Market Talk
data.description: Northern Star Resources's new bull lays out the case for the gold miner to sell high cost, shorter life assets. UBS upgrades Northern Star to buy, from sell, arguing a 40% fall in its stock price over the past month is overdone. Analyst Levi Spry says Northern Star could improve the quality of its portfolio through asset sales. Doing so could lower average all-in sustaining costs by A$200/oz, or 10%, and extend average asset life by four years, UBS says. "Based on domestic and global peer valuations, this higher-quality portfolio could justify up to additional 2 turns on its enterprise value-to-Ebitda multiple, partially offsetting lost earnings," UBS says. Also, proceeds from asset sales would likely fund share buybacks or dividends, potentially lifting the stock more. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Cayman's Oxbridge Re swings to Q4 profit
data.description:
Overview

  • Cayman Islands tokenized reinsurance platform's Q4 net premiums earned fell. Q4 Net income swung to profit

  • Full-year net loss narrowed as higher revenues offset increased expenses and reinsurance losses

  • Combined ratio rose to 264.1% for 2025, reflecting higher expenses and hurricane-related losses

Outlook

  • Oxbridge targets 20% and 42% annual returns for T20 and T42 tokens in 2026–2027

  • Company says El Niño may reduce hurricane activity for 2026 Atlantic season

  • Oxbridge plans to expand tokenization to data center revenues and AI infrastructure assets

Result Drivers

  • PREMIUM RATE DECLINE - Net premiums earned fell due to lower weighted average rates on reinsurance contracts in force during the quarter

  • HURRICANE LOSSES - Higher expenses and loss ratio driven by losses on reinsurance contracts affected by Hurricane Milton

  • HIGHER OPERATING COSTS - Expense ratio rose due to increased professional, marketing, tokenization, and legal costs

data.title: UBS Cuts Year-End Target for Australia's Benchmark ASX 200 — Market Talk
data.description: UBS lowers its year-end target for Australia's benchmark S&P/ASX 200 index. It cites a likelihood that inflation spikes and economic growth slows, but the country avoids a recession. UBS now expects the ASX 200 to end the year at 8800, compared to a prior forecast of 9400. The ASX 200 ended Monday at 8461. "Although earnings downgrades will likely dominate through the next quarter, markets react fast nowadays, and prices will choose to 'look through' to the other side," strategist Richard Schellbach says. "The EPS downgrades we expect over coming months should see the pace of profit growth decelerate." UBS's new 8800 target would mean the ASX 200 ends 2026 roughly flat for the year. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)
